title: Living in Boulder vlog 5 - 2 - 2017: Fixed Livy

enrichment.summary: Kyle describes working through the night on a Roman history timeline website, adding 7,500 date links and YouTube playlist icons. He plans to spend the next week or two attaching public-domain Latin historical texts (Livy, Tacitus's Annals and Histories, Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire) to the timeline, sourcing them from WikiSource and Project Gutenberg.
enrichment.speaker: kyle
topics: Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire

stances.position: Finds it beautiful how much Roman history was recorded across so many detailed books
Focused on attaching source texts to timeline before worrying about next steps; content to work and smoke rather than exercise

entities: Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was too hooked on my site yesterday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today I decided to do a new thing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was working on the Roman timeline and time chart yesterday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I attached 7,500 links to the Roman timeline from Mike Blog and Page and from the books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I also attached old YouTube icons for the playlist for my video and put a bit of page 2.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I worked until 6 o'clock in the morning yesterday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I thought I finished the work yesterday but didn't.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got up at 11 today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I worked on the site all day today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today I decided to work on the audio books for the timeline instead of the timeline itself.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I found more books on WikiSource than I originally had from Goonburg.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Some of the books on WikiSource are from people who knew the book or something.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm attaching books 11 through 20 to the timeline.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I won't be done with the work today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When I'm done with the books, I'll attach them to the timeline on the vertical part.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might also attach the books to part 2 of the timeline.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: After the current books, I'll work on Tassad's Annals, History's Allad, Ulysses, and Decline and Fall.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll attach all those books to the timeline on my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Livy's books cover 3,500 pages over 700 years or more.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Tacitus's Annals and Histories cover AD 9 to 98, which is only 29 years but has a lot of detail.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Decline and Fall is about 8,000 pages.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When all these books are attached to the timeline, I'll be closer to being ready to do my show.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Working through Decline and Fall might take me until I'm 90.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll spend the next week or two attaching those books to the timeline.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: After that, I'll finish the Google Earth stuff.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Once everything is attached, it'll be awesome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to start smoking again.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm not exercising, just working on the site.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm excited about the progress.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't need to think about what I'll do after finishing the current goals.
